title: A PARADE OF HORRIBLES
author: Matt Dinniman
description: The eighth book in the “Dungeon Crawler Carl” series. A sense of normalcy might be a set-up that spells danger for Carl and Donut.

title: THEO OF GOLDEN
author: Allen Levi
description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

title: YESTERYEAR
author: Caro Claire Burke
description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

title: REMARKABLY BRIGHT CREATURES
author: ShelVan Pelt
description: A widow working the night shift at the Sowell Bay Aquarium is aided in solving a mystery by a giant Pacific octopus living there; the basis of the movie.

title: PROJECT HAIL MARY
author: Andy Weir
description: Ryland Grace awakes from a long sleep alone and far from home, and the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders; the basis of the movie.

title: SUICIDAL EMPATHY
author: Gad Saad
description: The host of the podcast “The Saad Truth” argues that what he considers misguided compassion is having a negative impact on society.

title: STRANGERS
author: Belle Burden
description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward.

title: TAKE ME TO YOUR LEADER
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: The astrophysicist depicts what potential encounters with aliens from outer space might be like.

title: THE CASE FOR AMERICA
author: Bret Baier
description: The chief political anchor for Fox News shares his perspectives on our national character during divisive times.

title: FAMESICK
author: Lena Dunham
description: The author of “Not That Kind of Girl” evaluates the effects that pursuing her creative endeavors had on her.

title: OPERATION TRICK SHOT
author: Dude Perfect with Allan Woodrow. Illustrated Genevieve Kote
description: The gang organize a trick shot show to raise money for the neighborhood rec center. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: WONDER
author: R.J. Palacio
description: A boy with a facial deformity starts school. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: WOMBAT WAITING
author: Katherine Applegate
description: A homeless pup patiently waits for her “special person.” (Ages 8 to 12)

title: KPOP DEMON HUNTERS
author: Jessica Yoon
description: Huntrix takes on the Saja Boys, a boy band who happen to be demons in disguise. Based on the movie. (Ages 7 to 10)

title: UNSETTLING SALAD!
author: Aaron Reynolds. Illustrated Peter Brown
description: Thadeus and Oliver take a bite out of a salad that may bite back! (Ages 6 to 9)

title: HEROES OF 1776
author: Neil Gorsuch and Janie Nitze. Illustrated Chris Ellison
description: A brief history of the Declaration of Independence. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: BECAUSE I HAD A TEACHER
author: Kobi Yamada. Illustrated Natalie Russell
description: An ode to teachers. (Ages 4 to 7)

title: KINDERGARTEN GRADUATION!
author: Jennifer Liberts. Illustrated Courtney Dawson
description: Matteo graduates from kindergarten! (Ages 2 to 5)

title: I WISH YOU MORE
author: Amy Krouse Rosenthal. Illustrated Tom Lichtenheld
description: A bounty of good wishes. (Ages 5 to 8)

title: WHY A DAUGHTER NEEDS A DAD
author: Gregory E. Lang. Illustrated Sydney Hanson
description: A father's love for his daughter. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: THE HUNGER GAMES
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopia, a girl fights for survival on live TV. (Ages 12 and up)

title: DIARY OF A WIMPY KID
author: Jeff Kinney. Illustrated Jeff Kinney
description: The travails and challenges of adolescence. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: THE WILD ROBOT
author: Peter Brown
description: Roz the robot adapts to her surroundings on a remote, wild island. (Ages 7 to 12)

title: WINGS OF FIRE
author: Tui T. Sutherland
description: Only the five dragonets of destiny can unite the seven warring dragon tribes. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: HARRY POTTER
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A wizard hones his conjuring skills in the service of fighting evil. (Ages 10 and up)

title: THE ESCAPE GAME
author: Marissa Meyer and Tamara Moss
description: A real murder takes place on an escape room-themed game show. (Ages 14 and up)

title: RELEASE ME
author: Tahereh Mafi
description: The romantic tension mounts between Rosabelle and James as the fight in the New Republic continues. (Ages 14 and up)

title: FAKE SKATING
author: Lynn Painter
description: After years of separation, the childhood friends Alec and Dani reunite and face their true feelings toward each other. (Ages 14 and up)

title: CHANGE OF PLANS
author: Sarah Dessen
description: The coming-of-age love story of a girl named Finley, who goes on a last-minute trip with her distant mother. (Ages 12 and up)

title: IF ONLY I HAD TOLD HER
author: Laura Nowlin
description: In this companion to “If He Had Been With Me,” the story of Finn and Autumn is told through three different perspectives. (Ages 14 to 18)

title: THE LET THEM THEORY
author: Mel Robbins
description: Read by the author. 10 hours, 38 minutes unabridged.

title: ATOMIC HABITS
author: James Clear
description: Read by the author. 5 hours, 35 minutes unabridged.

title: THE MEANING OF YOUR LIFE
author: Arthur C. Brooks
description: Read by the author. 6 hours, 42 minutes unabridged.

title: THE 48 LAWS OF POWER
author: Robert Greene
description: Read by Richard Poe. 23 hours, 6 minutes unabridged.

title: START WITH YOURSELF
author: Emma Grede
description: Read by the author. 9 hours, 14 minutes unabridged.
